subject: ap-chemistry
title: Intermolecular Forces & Physical Properties
stimulus:
4. Answer the following questions about the compounds NH Cl and NCl . The Lewis electron-dot diagrams of the
two compounds are shown.

parts.prompt:
Calculate the number of moles of NH Cl (molar mass 51.48 g/mol) present in 1.0 L of a solution in which
the concentration of NH Cl is 0.0016 g/L.

scoring_points.description: For a correct calculated value: 1 point

required_elements: For a correct calculated value: 1 point

parts.prompt:
NH Cl is highly soluble in water, whereas NCl is nearly insoluble. Explain this observation in terms of the
types and relative strengths of the intermolecular forces between each of the solutes and water.

scoring_points.description: For the correct identification of intermolecular forces between each substance and water: 1 point

required_elements: Provide a correct response from the accepted alternatives
alternatives.correct_example: Both NH2Cl and NCl3 can participate in hydrogen bonding with water
The intermolecular forces between NH Cl molecules and water are stronger than those

parts.prompt:
The value of ∆H for NCl (l) is 32.9 kJ/mol. Calculate the amount of energy required to vaporize a
vaporization 3
15.0 g sample of NCl (molar mass 120.36 g/mol).

scoring_points.description: For the correct calculated value: 1 point

required_elements: For the correct calculated value: 1 point
alternatives.correct_example: 1 mol 32.9 kJ15.0 g NCl × × = 4.10 kJ3 120.36 g 1 mol Total for
